header.title: Images to PDF — Convert JPG, PNG & WebP to PDF Online
header.description: Free online tool to convert images to PDF. Combine JPG, PNG, WebP, and other image formats into a single PDF document. Arrange and reorder images before conversion — 100% client-side, your files never leave your browser.
header.keywords: images to pdf, convert image to pdf, jpg to pdf, png to pdf, webp to pdf, image to pdf converter, combine images to pdf, picture to pdf

privacy.notice: 100% client-side. Your images are converted entirely in the browser — nothing is uploaded to any server.

dropzone.imageFormats: JPG · PNG · WebP · GIF · BMP — multiple files

contain.label: Contain
contain.desc: Fit inside page, keep aspect ratio

empty.message: Add images above to combine into a PDF

howToUse.title: How to Use Images to PDF
howToUse.step1Desc: Drop or upload JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, or BMP images. You can add multiple files at once.
howToUse.step2Desc: Drag to reorder images. Choose page size, orientation, image fit, margins, and background color.
howToUse.step3Desc: Optionally enable page numbers, adjust image quality, and enter a custom output filename.
howToUse.step4Desc: Click 'Create PDF' and download your combined document — all processing happens in your browser.

items.question: What image formats are supported?
items.answer: JPG, PNG, WebP, GIF, and BMP are supported. All images are converted and embedded into a single PDF document.

items.question: Can I mix different image formats in one PDF?
items.answer: Yes. You can combine JPG, PNG, WebP, and other supported formats in any order within the same PDF.

items.question: How do I reorder images before conversion?
items.answer: Use the up/down arrows or drag and drop to rearrange images in the list before clicking 'Create PDF'.

items.question: What page sizes are available?
items.answer: A4, A3, Letter, Legal, and 'Fit to image size' which creates a page matching each image's dimensions.

items.question: Will my images be compressed?
items.answer: Images can be kept at original quality (larger PDF) or compressed (smaller PDF). The quality slider lets you control the JPEG compression level.

items.question: Is there a limit on the number of images?
items.answer: There is no hard limit, but browser memory may constrain very large batches (100+ high-resolution images).

items.question: Are my images uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs client-side in your browser. Your images never leave your device.
